name: Apple Fritters (Irish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the batter at least one hour before it is needed, using the following method: Sift together flour and salt. Make a well in the center. Add the cooled melted butter and some of the water and egg yolks. Work in the flour and beat until smooth. Add the remaining water. Leave to stand. Just before using, beat the egg whites until stiff but not dry. Fold into the batter mixture.
2. Peel, core, and slice the apples (slices about 1/4-1/2 inch thick). Dip each slice into the batter and deep fry in very hot oil (175-180°C) until golden brown.
3. Drain the fritters on paper towels and serve sprinkled with sugar and lemon juice.
